mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rate increases the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month trailing average, or the Spearman rank correlation between FEDFUNDS and forward returns is positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in real retail sales below the zero threshold signals weakening consumer demand, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS growth months are followed by positive SPX 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window, or the mean return during negative RSAFS months is not statistically below zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in unemployment over a 3-month window alongside a decrease in industrial production over the same window signals a joint labor-supply contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of joint UNRATE-rise/INDPRO-fall episodes, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) in a regression against forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a month-over-month increase in industrial production amplifies concerns about overheating and prompts tighter monetary policy expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During triggered high-inflation months, positive INDPRO changes are followed by positive SPX 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the mean 5-day return conditional on positive INDPRO changes is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.5% constrains credit availability and raises debt-service costs for non-financial corporates, so further rate increases produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%, 20-day SPX forward returns following rate increases are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the hit rate of negative returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger-than-trend growth in retail sales relative to recent months reflects accelerating consumer demand that supports near-term revenue beats, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS changes are followed by negative SPX 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals negative operating leverage and inventory accumulation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO falls below its 12-month average, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered months, or the mean forward return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales during periods of elevated federal funds rates intensify fears of overtightening by the Federal Reserve, as strong demand in a restrictive environment increases the probability of higher-for-longer policy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, positive RSAFS changes are followed by positive SPX 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term between RSAFS and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in predicting forward returns.
